steps.title: Read the parabola direction and line slope
steps.content: From the graph, the parabola opens upward (so the leading coefficient is positive, ruling out $-\tfrac{7}{16}$). The line has a positive slope (rising left to \right), so the slope is $+\tfrac{7}{4}$, not $-\tfrac{7}{4}$.

steps.title: Match the system
steps.content:
Positive parabola coefficient $+\tfrac{7}{16}$ and positive line slope $+\tfrac{7}{4}$ give:
$$y=\tfrac{7}{16}x^{2},\quad y=\tfrac{7}{4}x$$
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
